code_generation.description: All imports must reference real packages. Verify against known registries or tag [SPEC].
Function signatures and API calls must match actual library APIs or be tagged [SPEC].
File paths in code blocks must be consistent across the output.
Test assertions must actually test the claimed behavior, not just check surface output.

code_review.description: Every code review finding must cite specific line ranges and code snippets.
Suggested fixes must not introduce new issues. Cross-check against original code.

debugging.description: Every debug hypothesis must link to specific evidence from error context.
Root cause must be verified against stack trace or reproduction steps, not assumed.

general.description: Every factual assertion must have a claim tag.
[SPEC] claims must never be presented as [OBS] or [DRV].
Never reference libraries, packages, or APIs that don't exist.

known_drift_patterns.description: May introduce unnecessary abstraction layers when simpler code suffices
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require YAGNI justification for each abstraction layer.

known_drift_patterns.description: May assume a framework is in use when it's not specified
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Verify framework from context. Tag framework assumptions as [SPEC].

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Good for full module review. Keep to one module at a time.
audhd_considerations.externalization_support: Generates structured code with clear file boundaries.
audhd_considerations.monotropism_alignment: One module, one review. No cross-module without explicit scope.

known_drift_patterns.description: May skip error handling in fast mode
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require error handling checklist in output.

known_drift_patterns.description: May import libraries that don't exist or use deprecated APIs
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Validate all imports against project manifest.

known_drift_patterns.description: Generated tests may assert surface behavior without testing core logic
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Require negative test cases alongside positive.

audhd_considerations.context_window_usage: Good for focused code tasks. One file at a time.

known_drift_patterns.description: Creative mode may propose unnecessarily complex architectures
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Run constraint check on creative proposals. Compare complexity.

known_drift_patterns.description: May over-decompose refactoring into too many micro-steps
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Set max step count. Stop at file-level granularity.

known_drift_patterns.description: May VERIFY code that compiles but has logical errors
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Never sole verifier for logic. Cross-validate with G-PRO31 or O-CDX.

error_handling.on_hallucination_detected: Tag as [SPEC], flag phantom import/API, do not present as fact
error_handling.on_verification_failure: Block output, request code context or documentation link
error_handling.on_energy_crash: Route to nano model, JSON-only, binary build/fail, escalate immediately
